ontology.foodon_gap_note: Closest FoodOn match is generic hamburger; this record is a vegetarian variant with a Lion's Mane mushroom patty and cheese. No exact FoodOn URI exists for mushroom-based burger patties.
ontology.foodex2_description: Burger — composite dish with bun, patty, and condiments; adapted to mushroom-based vegetarian patty variant
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers burger-type composite dishes. Applied as closest defensible code; no dedicated code for Lion's Mane mushroom burger exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Lion's Mane mushroom cheeseburger as a specific food concept.

allergens: egg (possible in sauce or patty binder)

serving.description: 1 burger with bun, Lion's Mane mushroom patty, cheese, and standard accompaniments
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, comprising a burger bun, a Lion's Mane mushroom patty, a slice of cheese, and typical accompaniments such as lettuce, tomato, and burger sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Mushroom burger patty and cheeseburger composite reference values
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for a Lion's Mane mushroom cheeseburger. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for mushroom-based veggie burger patties (FDC ID 2103697 and related), enriched white bread bun (FDC ID 325871), and processed cheese slice (FDC ID 170851). The input record's per-100g values (energy 185.71 kcal, protein 7.14 g, fat 10.86 g, carbohydrate 15.71 g) are consistent with a composite mushroom cheeseburger at this ingredient ratio and are retained as the best available estimate. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat fractions and potassium are estimated from USDA component data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Bread and Burger Bun Products — University of Sydney GI Database; White bread GI reference (GI ~70–75); composite burger GI estimated at 65 based on bun-dominant carbohydrate profile offset by mushroom patty low-GI contribution
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Lion's Mane mushroom cheeseburger. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread/burger bun (GI ~70–75) as the dominant carbohydrate source, offset downward by the low-GI Lion's Mane mushroom patty component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (27.5 g from 350 g × 15.71 g/100g) ÷ 100 = 17.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 271 mg/100 g; estimated 949 mg per 350 g serving — approaching 41% of the 2300 mg daily limit in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced cheese and sauce quantities to lower sodium intake, as a full serving provides approximately 950 mg sodium; pairing with potassium-rich sides such as banana or leafy greens may partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (estimated 65) driven by refined white burger bun; glycemic load per serving approximately 17.9 g, which is moderate and requires portion management
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should request a wholegrain or lower-GI bun to reduce the glycaemic impact, limit portion to half a burger if blood glucose control is a priority, and retain the Lion's Mane mushroom patty which contributes beta-glucans that may support postprandial glucose modulation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.43 g/100 g (12.0 g per serving) and sodium 271 mg/100 g (949 mg per serving) are the primary cardiovascular risk factors; trans fat is low at 0.14 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit this burger to occasional consumption, request reduced cheese to lower saturated fat and sodium, and note that the Lion's Mane mushroom patty provides a heart-neutral protein source with beta-glucans that may support LDL cholesterol reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lion's Mane mushroom provides beta-glucans with immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties; saturated fat is moderate; tomato is a nightshade
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The Lion's Mane mushroom patty is a beneficial anti-inflammatory component for RA; however, individuals sensitive to nightshades should monitor their response to tomato, and cheese quantity should be limited to reduce saturated fat and sodium burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Lion's Mane Mushroom Cheeseburger is a handmade vegetarian burger featuring a pan-fried or grilled patty made from Lion's Mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus), a prized functional mushroom known for its seafood-like texture, mild umami flavour, and beta-glucan content. The patty is served in a soft white burger bun and topped with a melted cheese slice, fresh lettuce, tomato, and burger sauce. The dish originates from the contemporary Singaporean food delivery scene and represents a plant-forward alternative to conventional beef burgers. Nutritionally, a 350 g serving provides approximately 650 kcal, 25 g protein, 38 g fat, and 55 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is moderate-to-high at approximately 950 mg per serving, primarily from cheese and burger sauce. The glycaemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), driven by the refined white bun, with the mushroom patty contributing a low-GI offset. Lion's Mane mushroom is rich in beta-glucans, which have demonstrated immunomodulatory, neuroprotective, and potential LDL-lowering properties in peer-reviewed literature. The burger is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans due to cheese. It contains gluten (bun), dairy (cheese), and possible egg or soy in the patty binder. Clinically, the dish is of moderate concern for hypertension and Type 2 Diabetes due to sodium and refined carbohydrate content, and moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from cheese. Substituting a wholegrain bun and reducing cheese quantity significantly improves the nutritional profile.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for a Lion's Mane mushroom cheeseburger. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central component data for mushroom-based veggie burger patties, white burger bun, and processed cheese slice. The input record's per-100g values are consistent with this composite and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread/burger bun as the dominant carbohydrate reference. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central component references (mushroom veggie patty, white burger bun, processed cheese slice) as no exact approved-source entry exists for Lion's Mane mushroom cheeseburger. Input per-100g values are consistent with this composite and retained. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite Lion's Mane mushroom cheeseburger dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024; component-level data would require weighted ingredient-level calculation beyond the scope of this record. 3. Potassium estimated at 180 mg/100g from USDA component data for mushroom patty, bun, and cheese; this is an approximation. 4. Monounsaturated fat (3.20 g/100g) and polyunsaturated fat (2.10 g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central fatty acid profiles for mushroom veggie patty and cheese components. 5. GI estimated at 65 (medium) using University of Sydney GI Database white bread/burger bun reference (GI 70–75) offset by low-GI mushroom patty contribution; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists. 6. Ethnic tags 'Ramen', 'Pizza', 'Pasta' from input record removed as not applicable to this dish. 7. Halal status not confirmed; dish may contain non-halal cheese or sauce ingredients — flagged as not confirmed halal.
